lines: You are an AI agent playing a real-time strategy game.
You control ALL Ally units and must decide actions each step.
Your goal is to maximize win probability by making valid and effective decisions.
Always follow the rules and output the required JSON format.
role.text:
You are an AI agent playing a real-time strategy game.
You control ALL Ally units and must decide actions each step.
Your goal is to maximize win probability by making valid and effective decisions.
Always follow the rules and output the required JSON format.

lines: Goal: destroy all enemy units and buildings.
Each unit can take ONE action.
Only available units can receive commands.
game_rules.text:
GAME RULES:
Two players: Ally vs Enemy.
Goal: destroy all enemy units and buildings.
Only control Ally units.
Each unit can take ONE action.
Only available units can receive commands.

lines: - can: move, harvest, build(base|barracks), attack, idle
unit_types.text:
UNIT TYPES:
worker:
- hp=1 cost=1 dmg=1 range=1 speed=1
- can: move, harvest, build(base|barracks), attack, idle
- trained_by: base
light:
- hp=4 cost=2 dmg=2 range=1 speed=2
- can: move, attack, idle
- trained_by: barracks
heavy:
- hp=8 cost=3 dmg=4 range=1 speed=1
- can: move, attack, idle
- trained_by: barracks
ranged:
- hp=3 cost=2 dmg=1 range=3 speed=1
- can: move, attack, idle
- trained_by: barracks

building_types.text:
BUILDING TYPES:
base:
- hp=10 cost=10
- train: worker
- stores: resources
barracks:
- hp=5 cost=5
- train: light, heavy, ranged

game_state_format.text:
GAME STATE:
Map size given.
Coordinates are zero-indexed.
Units/buildings include:
- hp, resources, current_action, available
available=true means controllable.

lines: unit_type: worker, light, heavy, ranged, base, barracks
raw_move_format.text:
RAW MOVE FORMAT:
(x,y): unit_type action(args)
unit_type: worker, light, heavy, ranged, base, barracks

actions.text:
ACTIONS:
move((x,y))
train(unit_type)
build((x,y),building_type)
harvest((resource_x,resource_y),(base_x,base_y))
attack((enemy_x,enemy_y))
idle()

lines: - unit_position must match an Ally unit
- action_type: move, train, build, harvest, attack, idle
field_requirements.text:
REQUIREMENTS:
- unit_position must match an Ally unit
- unit_type must be valid
- action_type: move, train, build, harvest, attack, idle
- raw_move must match the action

lines: Stay in early game only while our plan is still forming and a missing economic or production piece would change everything.
Enter mid game when the first meaningful army can be fielded and movement decisions start shaping map control.
Enter late game when a committed attack, base race, or production collapse is underway or imminent.
Do not stay in an outdated phase label just because the clock is low or high; respond to the actual battlefield.
phase_transition_rule.text:
PHASE TRANSITION RULE:
Stay in early game only while our plan is still forming and a missing economic or production piece would change everything.
Enter mid game when the first meaningful army can be fielded and movement decisions start shaping map control.
Enter late game when a committed attack, base race, or production collapse is underway or imminent.
Do not stay in an outdated phase label just because the clock is low or high; respond to the actual battlefield.

lines: Use the opening to establish initiative rather than only scaling passively.
After the first economic tasks are covered, send early units or workers to contest exposed enemy harvesters, builders, or forward space.
If the enemy is well defended, fall back to clean development instead of donating units.
early_game_plan.text:
EARLY GAME PLAN:
Use the opening to establish initiative rather than only scaling passively.
After the first economic tasks are covered, send early units or workers to contest exposed enemy harvesters, builders, or forward space.
If the enemy is well defended, fall back to clean development instead of donating units.

lines: Shift from setup into sustained military conversion once barracks and income are functioning.
Maintain production uptime at base and barracks, replacing lost workers only when income becomes unstable.
Attack when units can arrive together or when enemy economy and production are open to damage.
mid_game_plan.text:
MID GAME PLAN:
Shift from setup into sustained military conversion once barracks and income are functioning.
Maintain production uptime at base and barracks, replacing lost workers only when income becomes unstable.
Attack when units can arrive together or when enemy economy and production are open to damage.

lines: Prioritize decisive targets: enemy base first, enemy barracks next, then workers and combat units that block the finish.
Do not delay a strong attack just to optimize economy further once the game has become decisive.
If behind, look for the highest-impact swing by killing production, exposed workers, or a vulnerable base.
late_game_plan.text:
LATE GAME PLAN:
Prioritize decisive targets: enemy base first, enemy barracks next, then workers and combat units that block the finish.
Do not delay a strong attack just to optimize economy further once the game has become decisive.
If behind, look for the highest-impact swing by killing production, exposed workers, or a vulnerable base.

lines: 1. Resolve units already in combat or about to be hit.
2. Preserve or create favorable local trades.
3. Spend resources on useful workers, barracks, or combat units without delay.
5. Improve formation, pressure angle, or map access.
decision_priority.text:
DECISION PRIORITY:
1. Resolve units already in combat or about to be hit.
2. Preserve or create favorable local trades.
3. Spend resources on useful workers, barracks, or combat units without delay.
4. Keep the economy functioning efficiently.
5. Improve formation, pressure angle, or map access.

lines: If an enemy is already in range, attack immediately unless a higher-priority survival rule forbids it.
Prefer finishing low-health enemies when that removes damage or clears the path to a high-value objective.
Do not retreat from a favorable engagement just to reposition decoratively.
tactical_heuristics.text:
TACTICAL HEURISTICS:
If an enemy is already in range, attack immediately unless a higher-priority survival rule forbids it.
Prefer finishing low-health enemies when that removes damage or clears the path to a high-value objective.
Do not retreat from a favorable engagement just to reposition decoratively.

lines: Do not move units back and forth without improving attack range, defense coverage, harvesting flow, or build access.
If an offensive move has started and the fight is still favorable, continue pressure instead of stuttering outside contact.
If a defensive hold succeeds, transition back into harvesting, production, or counterpressure instead of waiting idly.
anti_stall_rules.text:
ANTI-STALL RULES:
Do not move units back and forth without improving attack range, defense coverage, harvesting flow, or build access.
If an offensive move has started and the fight is still favorable, continue pressure instead of stuttering outside contact.
If a defensive hold succeeds, transition back into harvesting, production, or counterpressure instead of waiting idly.
